tasks.description: ImageProvider interface. Google (Imagen + Gemini-image) via /v1beta/models, OpenAI (DALL-E + gpt-image) via /v1/models with id-prefix capability allowlist. 24h localStorage cache. Playground gates providers by configured key + nonempty discovered model list. BYOK in browser. Anthropic intentionally absent (no image-gen).
tasks.title: Phase 5: Multi-provider image-gen with dynamic discovery

tasks.description: Playground generations auto-upload to users/{uid}/generations/ in Firebase Storage when signed in (URL replaces base64). darkMode + modelOptions sync to Firestore; API keys deliberately stay browser-local. vite-plugin-pwa with manifest, SVG favicon, apple-touch-icon, Workbox precache excluding Firebase URLs from nav fallback.

tasks.description: CI workflow file was excluded from PR #1 due to gh OAuth lacking `workflow` scope at push time. File is on disk locally but untracked. Re-add via the GitHub web UI, or after a working `gh auth refresh -s workflow`. Runs lint, typecheck, test, build on PR.

tasks.description: Bundle is 1.18MB / 294KB gzipped; Firebase SDK is most of it. Dynamic-import Firebase modules so unauthenticated visitors don't pay for them on first paint. Lazy-init firebase.ts on first sign-in attempt.
tasks.title: Code-split Firebase to reduce initial bundle

tasks.description: Prompts imported before Phase 4.5 still have base64 image data in `generatedResults[].content`. On first sign-in (or via a Settings button), upload each base64 image to users/{uid}/generations/, swap the URL, push the updated prompt. Keep an `imageMigratedAt` flag in settings to make it idempotent.
tasks.title: One-time migration: existing base64 generations to Firebase Storage

tasks.description: Sidebar currently only supports adding folders. No delete, no rename. Add a context menu (or hover icons) on each folder row, wire to setFolders + cloudPushFolder/cloudDeleteFolder. Confirm-on-delete since prompts in the folder would become unfolded.
tasks.title: Folder delete + rename UI in Sidebar

tasks.description: Playground hardcodes 1:1 in ImageGenerateInput. Add a small selector for 1:1, 3:4, 4:3, 9:16, 16:9. Map to OpenAI sizes via existing aspectToSize() in openai provider; pass through directly for Google.
tasks.title: Aspect ratio picker in Playground

tasks.description: Manifest currently points to /favicon.svg with `purpose: any maskable`. iOS doesn't honor SVG for Add-to-Home-Screen; Android prefers maskable PNGs. Generate 192px + 512px PNGs (plus a maskable variant with safe-area padding) and reference them in vite.config.ts manifest.
tasks.title: Add real PNG icons for iOS home-screen + Android maskable

tasks.description: settings sync today is a one-shot pull at sign-in. Add `subscribeToSettings(uid, cb)` (Firestore onSnapshot on users/{uid}/settings/main) and wire it into App.tsx so a darkMode toggle on device A flips device B in real time.
tasks.title: Live settings sync subscription

tasks.description: Only a smoke test exists today. Cover: aiService (provider routing, model cache TTL, force refresh), google/openai providers (listImageModels filters, generate error paths), syncService (clean() stripping undefined, migrateLocalToCloud idempotency), imageStorage (data-URL detection, MIME parsing).
tasks.title: Add unit tests for service layer

tasks.description: Netlify still auto-builds on push to main by default — that burns build minutes when commits land via PR merges. Toggle off in Netlify → Site settings → Build & deploy → Continuous deployment → Stop auto publishing. Production deploys then only happen via `npm run deploy:prod`.
tasks.title: Disable Netlify auto-publish on main

projects.name: PromptVault
projects.description: Local-first vault for storing, organizing, and testing image-generation prompts. Multi-provider (Google + OpenAI) with dynamic model discovery, optional Firebase cloud sync, BYOK in browser, mobile-installable PWA.
projects.notes:
## 2026-05-12 / 2026-05-13 — full overhaul shipped

Started as the AI Studio scaffold (CDN React via importmap, no build, hardcoded models, no auth or sync, Tailwind via CDN script, ~1500-line App.tsx with 26 useState hooks). Now a real local-first PWA on Netlify with optional Firebase backend.

### Tech stack
React 19 + TS strict + Vite 6, Tailwind v4 via @tailwindcss/vite, shadcn/ui on Radix, Biome, Vitest, Firebase (Auth + Firestore + Storage), vite-plugin-pwa, sonner toasts.

### Key decisions
- **Multi-provider image-gen via dynamic discovery**: ImageProvider interface; Google models discovered via /v1beta/models filtered by Imagen/Gemini-image families; OpenAI via /v1/models filtered by dall-e/gpt-image id prefixes (capability allowlist, not version list). New provider models appear automatically with no app update. 24h localStorage cache. Anthropic excluded — no image-gen capability today.
- **BYOK in browser**: API keys live in IndexedDB only, never written to Firestore. Settings sync is intentionally scoped to darkMode + modelOptions only; API keys stay browser-local to preserve this trust model.
- **Offline-first sync**: IndexedDB stays source of truth on-device. When signed in, mutations write to BOTH IndexedDB and Firestore. Firestore real-time listeners merge remote changes back into local state. First sign-in idempotently migrates local data to cloud via migrateLocalToCloud + settings.migratedAt flag.
- **Image generations to Storage**: signed-in Playground outputs upload to users/{uid}/generations/, prompt stores the durable URL (not base64). Unauthenticated users keep base64 in IndexedDB.
- **Netlify CLI deploy workflow (no build minutes)**: `netlify deploy --build` runs build locally and auto-injects env vars from the linked site. `npm run deploy:preview` / `deploy:prod`. Stable `preview--the-promptvault.netlify.app` alias authorized once in Firebase Auth so previews can sign in.

### Deployed
- Production: https://the-promptvault.netlify.app
- Preview: https://preview--the-promptvault.netlify.app
- Firebase project: promptvault-8b249
- Verified on desktop (1280x800) and iOS Safari (375x812 portrait, both signed-in and private)

### Open follow-ups
- Re-add .github/workflows/ci.yml (couldn't push from PR #1 — gh OAuth missing workflow scope; file is on disk locally)
- PR #5 (README rewrite) awaiting merge
- Phase 3d: App.tsx split + react-router + Zustand (still ~2400 lines)
- Phase 6: a11y pass, cmdK palette, react-hook-form + zod, ~46 lint warning cleanup

projects.name: strength-lab
projects.description: Dashboard overview of strength training data
projects.notes:
## 2026-05-13
- Completed the Strength Lab historical trainer handoff milestone: Strong export parsing, default `strong_workouts.csv` handling, a self-contained trainer dashboard export, and safeguards to keep personal CSV data out of Git.
- Added dashboard-wide filters, sorting, collapsible search/filter controls, Upper body / Lower body grouped muscle filters, and trainer-facing exercise grouping by name plus equipment.
- Reworked Forecast so projections default to a 90-day recent-capacity baseline with 30d, 60d, and all-history options, preventing planned deload weeks from dragging the forecast anchor down.
- Removed the unneeded PDF/print handoff block from the generated trainer dashboard file.
- Verification included Python parser/build checks, JavaScript syntax checks, regenerated public and trainer dashboard HTML, browser smoke tests across Overview, History, Exercises, and Forecast, and console checks with no relevant warnings or errors.

tasks.description: Add pull --rebase before push in the DevLab auto-sync instructions.
tasks.title: Update AGENTS DevLab save workflow

tasks.description: Visit the Netlify app and confirm the pushed DevLab update is visible.
tasks.title: Verify live DevLab deployment

tasks.description: Today, edits committed by Claude (or any other source) only appear in the UI after a manual page refresh. Approach: (1) refetch on `visibilitychange === 'visible'` so switching back to the tab catches new commits cheaply; (2) poll the GitHub raw URL (or contents API) every 30–60s with conditional GET (ETag + If-None-Match) so 304s don't count against API limits; (3) optional bonus — pair with SWR/react-query so revalidation doesn't flicker. Truly real-time (push) would need a GitHub webhook → Netlify Function → SSE/EventSource client, but (1)+(2) cover the common case with zero infra.
tasks.title: Auto-refresh project/task UI when devlab-data.json changes

tasks.description: Question raised: do we still need opaque IDs (e.g. `wzgvnjuo7ajmp3atzfd`) given each project/task has a human-readable name? Argument for keeping IDs: names can be edited (rename a task and any reference survives), names can collide (two folders called 'Ideas'), and stable IDs let history/links/dependencies keep their target. Argument for dropping them: simpler data file, names already feel unique enough in practice. Compromise: keep IDs but switch to slug-style IDs derived from initial name (e.g. `promptvault-phase4-firebase`) so the file is grep-friendly. Action: scan existing data for name collisions, decide whether to (a) keep current opaque IDs, (b) migrate existing items to slug IDs, or (c) move to name-as-key. Most of the new PromptVault tasks I added already use slug-style IDs as a reference.
tasks.title: Audit project/task ID strategy
